case_name: GARY L. MARDER, D.O. v. ROBERTA MUELLER

summary.brief: GARY L. MARDER, D.O. v. ROBERTA MUELLER. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Patient filed the underlying action in connection with Doctor’s treatment of a lesion on her hand.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Another physician diagnosed this lesion as squamous cell carcinoma following a biopsy, then made a referral to Doctor for treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Patient opted for radiation. As part of obtaining Patient’s informed consent, Doctor told her that while surgery was an option it would likely impact Patient’s ability to maintain her current lifestyle as an avid golfer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Doctor allegedly prescribed this protocol to all patients undergoing radiation therapy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: an attestation by her expert stating Doctor’s treatment fell “way outside” the standard of care
evidence_index.speaker: Patient's expert

evidence_index.text: We reverse because Patient failed to satisfy the requirements of section 768.72(1), Florida Statutes (2018).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Patient did not proffer sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Doctor’s conduct amounted to intentional misconduct or gross negligence
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Patient’s proffered evidence provided no reasonable basis for recovery of punitive damages
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The expert opined that radiation should be done over an extended time period to not expose a patient to excessive radiation
evidence_index.speaker: Patient's expert

evidence_index.text: Doctor’s radiation protocol fell “way outside” the standard of care, and he was not aware of any medical professional or peer-reviewed medical journal that condoned Doctor’s treatment plan.
evidence_index.speaker: Patient's expert

evidence_index.text: Doctor’s deposition testimony indicated he had personally lectured on his radiation treatment protocol to other dermatologists
evidence_index.speaker: Doctor

evidence_index.text: Patient did not proffer anything to suggest that Doctor knew his treatment plan for Patient was unnecessary or likely to cause serious injury
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Patient did not provide a link between either Doctor’s billing practices with other patients or his interference in an unrelated criminal healthcare investigation with any improper motivation for prescribing radiation treatment for Patient.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Based on the foregoing, we find Patient’s request to amend to state a claim for punitive damages regarding Patient’s alleged improper radiation treatment falls short of satisfying the standard required to assert such a claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with a lesion on her hand, which was diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma by another physician following a biopsy.

focused_exam.hand: Lesion present, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Lesion on hand diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma

requestables.name: Oncology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with oncologist regarding treatment options for squamous cell carcinoma

reveal.result_summary: Patient opted for radiation therapy after discussion of treatment options
reveal.clinical_significance: Informed consent obtained for radiation therapy
reveal.decision_impact: Patient chose radiation over surgery to maintain lifestyle

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a lesion on her hand

key_events.event: Lesion diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with squamous cell carcinoma on hand
decision_points.prompt: What treatment protocol should be followed for radiation therapy?

options.description: Standard radiation protocol over an extended period
options.clinical_reasoning: Reduces risk of excessive radiation exposure

options.description: Radiation twice daily with treatments as little as forty-five minutes apart
options.clinical_reasoning: Protocol not recognized as acceptable in the medical community

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant used a non-standard radiation protocol
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed it was appropriate despite lack of medical community support

expected_action_court.description: Standard radiation protocol should have been used

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Protocol fell 'way outside' the standard of care
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Standard protocol should have been followed

patient_outcome.description: Increased risk of cancer due to radiation protocol

key_findings: Patient failed to satisfy requirements for punitive damages
Doctor's conduct did not amount to intentional misconduct or gross negligence

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Standard protocol would have reduced risk

factual_timeline.event: Lesion diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma

court_findings.finding: Patient failed to satisfy requirements for punitive damages
